questions.text: The juggler in the photograph rotates the umbrella in such a way that the disk rolling on the umbrella stays in the same place in the room reference frame, as shown: The coefficient of a rolling friction C is a property of a rolling object and the surface on which it rolls. It is defined by the equation F = CN, where F is the magnitude of the applied force required for the object to roll at a constant velocity, and N is the magnitude of the normal force of the object relative to the surface.

parts.label: a
parts.text: Make appropriate measurements on the photo and some approximations to figure out what is the coefficient of rolling friction of the disk over the umbrella material.

parts.label: b
parts.text: Using the photo can you determine the angular velocity of the rotation of the umbrella?

questions.given_data: F = CN (coefficient of rolling friction equation)
questions.diagram_description: Two photographs showing a juggler rotating an umbrella with a disk rolling on it. The right image shows the path of the disk in the umbrella frame of reference, depicted as a circular path on the umbrella surface.
questions.subject: physics

questions.text: Birefringence is an optical phenomena where a birefringent crystal refracts light in two different directions, resulting in the separation of a single incident light beam into two polarized rays with different refractive indices (no and ne). These two rays are often called the ordinary ray (o-ray) and the extraordinary ray (e-ray). Each ray travels through the crystal at a different speed. When an electromagnetic wave propagates in a medium with a refractive index n, the change in oscillation phase after going through a distance d can be expressed as follows: φ = 2πnd/λ

parts.label: a
parts.text: Suppose we have a birefringent crystal with thickness d. If a beam of light with a wavelength of λ propagates through the crystal, express the phase difference between the ordinary and extraordinary ray, φ, in terms of λ, ne, no, and d.

parts.label: b
parts.text: Write down the conditions for the phase difference, φ, that causes the transmitted light to have: i. Maximum intensity ii. Minimum intensity

parts.label: c
parts.text: It is known that the wavelengths for three primary colors are: – λred = 680 nm – λgreen = 530 nm – λblue = 470 nm Find the minimum thickness of the crystal, so that the transmitted light: i. Appears blue. ii. Appears yellow.

parts.label: d
parts.text: Write down the conditions for the phase difference, φ, that causes the transmitted light to have: i. Maximum intensity ii. Minimum intensity

parts.label: e
parts.text: Find the minimum thickness of the crystal, so that the transmitted light: i. Appears blue. ii. Appears yellow.

questions.given_data: φ = 2πnd/λ; A beam of linearly polarized polychromatic light, whose electric field's vector makes an angle θ = 45° with the x-axis, falls normally on a crystal. The refractive index for the electric field component in the x-direction is no = 2.647, and the refractive index for the electric field component in the y-direction is ne = 2.693.
questions.diagram_description: Multiple diagrams showing: 1) A birefringent crystal with optical axis, showing incident light splitting into o-ray and e-ray, 2) Crystal setup with coordinate axes and polarization components, 3) Polarizer arrangement diagrams showing incident light, crystal, and polarizers with various orientations
questions.subject: physics

questions.text: Two uncharged 10cm × 10cm metal plates, separated by a distance d = 1 mm are just touching the surface of water as shown in the figure. The electric permittivity of water is 10 times than that of the air. If a constant voltage difference of V = 600V is applied to the plates, determine how much the water rises inside the plates.
questions.given_data: d = 1 mm, V = 600V, εwater = 10εair, plate dimensions: 10cm × 10cm
questions.diagram_description: Diagram showing two parallel metal plates separated by distance d, with voltage V applied between them. The plates are positioned just touching the surface of water, with water level indicated.
questions.subject: physics
